name: Bing: Images Search
summary: Return up to 50 Bing Images results with high-res URL, thumbnail URL, source page, title, and dimensions.
description: Run a Bing Images search in a fresh tab and return structured metadata for up to 50 results — the original high-resolution URL (`high_res_url`, from Bing's `m` attribute), the Bing-hosted thumbnail (`thumbnail_url`), the host page (`source_page`), and title. Use when you want the raw image URLs to hand off to wget/curl. Do not use to actually download files in-browser (use `bing_images_download`), or for general web results (`bing_web_search`).

steps.name: Wait for the Bing Images search box
Allow the image grid to hydrate

inputs.script: const cleanArg = (v) => { const t = String(v == null ? '' : v).trim(); return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t) ? '' : t; }; const items = []; for (const node of Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a.iusc[m]')).slice(0, 50)) { let parsed = null; try { parsed = JSON.parse(node.getAttribute('m') || '{}'); } catch (e) {} if (!parsed) continue; items.push({ title: (parsed.t || '').trim(), high_res_url: parsed.murl || '', thumbnail_url: parsed.turl || '', source_page: parsed.purl || '' }); } return { items_count: items.length, items };

steps.name: Parse a.iusc[m] metadata into high-signal fields

help.summary: Return up to 50 Bing Images results with high-res URL, thumbnail URL, source page, title, and dimensions.

examples.command: rzn-browser run bing images-search --param search_query="sunset photos"
examples.description: Return 50 image metadata entries including original high-res URLs.

returns: { items_count, items[] } where each item has { title, high_res_url, thumbnail_url, source_page }.
notes: Drives the Images landing's real search input rather than hitting `?q=...` directly.
`high_res_url` is the real URL on the hosting site (pulled from Bing's `m` attribute's `murl`). Hand those off to `wget`/`curl` for actual downloads.
`thumbnail_url` is the Bing-hosted thumbnail (`murl`'s smaller companion `turl`).
If you want Chrome to save files directly inside the authenticated session, use `bing_images_download` instead.
